title: Prim, Kruskal, Wilson, Bias, and Distribution Claims · Program Defense
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss decisions for prim, kruskal, wilson, bias, and distribution claims and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit limitations, and named browser, Tcl, Tk, display, input, assistive-technology, file, package, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for comparative seeded generation laboratory. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and e…
objectives: Generate a spanning tree from a randomized frontier while preventing duplicate or stale frontier choices.
Shuffle candidate walls and use disjoint sets to admit only edges joining different components.
Construct a uniform spanning tree with loop-erased random walks and state termination assumptions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Select a generator from player, performance, reproducibility, topology, and accessibility goals with explicit non-guarantees.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Generator Selection, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Generator Selection precondition, or invariant that must hold.

Build deterministic Python evidence to compare multiple generators across seeded topology and bias evidence without unsupported distribution claims; event-loop work must remain cancellable; then replace pointer input with a single-action keyboard path.
